scenes.title: Animated Curve
scenes.description: Tests the `animated_curve` element: a y=f(x) curve with optional `fill_regions` for shading areas. Use the Doc tab for full reference.
scenes.markdown:
# Animated Curve

`animated_curve` plots a function $y = f(x)$ over a range and optionally shades **fill regions** between the curve and one or more boundary expressions. All fields are live â€” driven by sliders, time $t$, or scene functions.

---

## Minimal example

```json
{
  "type": "animated_curve",
  "expr": "sin(x)",
  "range": [-3.14159, 3.14159],
  "color": "#4da6ff"
}
```

---

## Full field reference

| Field | Default | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `expr` | `"0"` | Expression for $y = f(x)$. Variable `x` is injected automatically. |
| `range` | `[-1, 1]` | `[xMin, xMax]` â€” numbers or expression strings driven by sliders / $t$ |
| `samples` | `200` | Number of sample points along $x$ |
| `color` | `"#ff8844"` | Curve line color |
| `width` | `3` | Line width in pixels |
| `opacity` | `1` | Curve opacity â€” number or expression string |
| `showCurve` | `true` | Set `false` to hide the line and show fills only |
| `fill_regions` | `[]` | List of fill region objects (see below) |

---

## `fill_regions`

Each entry in `fill_regions` shades an area relative to the curve.

```json
"fill_regions": [
  {
    "id": "my-fill",
    "color": "#4488ff",
    "opacity": 0.4,
    "above": "0",
    "rightOf": "a",
    "leftOf": "b"
  }
]
```

### Fill region fields

| Field | Default | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `id` | â€” | Optional identifier |
| `color` | inherits curve color | Fill color |
| `opacity` | `0.35` | Fill opacity â€” number or expression string |
| `above` | â€” | Fill where curve $\geq g(x)$. $g(x)$ is the lower boundary; fill spans $[g(x), \text{curve}]$ |
| `below` | â€” | Fill where curve $\leq h(x)$. $h(x)$ is the upper boundary; fill spans $[\text{curve}, h(x)]$ |
| `rightOf` | â€” | Fill where $x \geq$ this value (expression). Equivalent to a left x-boundary. |
| `leftOf` | â€” | Fill where $x \leq$ this value (expression). Equivalent to a right x-boundary. |
| `outlineWidth` | â€” | Width (px) of a line drawn along the boundary expression (`above`/`below`). Number or expression string. **Omit for no boundary.** |
| `outlineColor` | inherits `color` | Boundary line color |
| `outlineOpacity` | `min(1, opacity Ă— 2)` | Boundary line opacity â€” number or expression string |

### Boundary semantics

| Boundaries specified | Region filled |
|---|---|
| `above` only | Between $g(x)$ and curve, where curve $\geq g(x)$ |
| `below` only | Between curve and $h(x)$, where curve $\leq h(x)$ |
| Both `above` and `below` | Curve clipped to the band $[g(x), h(x)]$ |
| Neither | Between $y=0$ and curve (above and below the axis) |

The curve is **always one boundary** of the filled area. `above`/`below` supply the opposite boundary.

`above` and `below` may depend on `x` (e.g. `"cos(x) - 1"`), enabling **fill between two curves**.

When `outlineWidth` is set, a MathBox line is drawn along the full perimeter of the fill region. **Default: no outline.**

---

## Recipes

### Fill between curve and x-axis

```json
"fill_regions": [
  { "color": "#4488ff", "opacity": 0.4, "above": "0" },
  { "color": "#ff4455", "opacity": 0.4, "below": "0" }
]
```

Blue above zero, red below â€” two regions, each specifying one boundary.

### Definite integral shading

```json
"fill_regions": [
  { "color": "#88ffcc", "opacity": "fill_op", "rightOf": "a", "leftOf": "b" }
]
```

Sliders `a` and `b` control the integration window. No `above`/`below` â†’ fills between curve and $y=0$.

### Fill between two curves

```json
{
  "expr": "sin(x)",
  "fill_regions": [
    { "color": "#aa88ff", "opacity": 0.4, "above": "cos(x) - 1" }
  ]
}
```

Shades where $\sin x \geq \cos x - 1$. The `above` expression is evaluated at each sample $x$.

### Animated range

```json
{
  "expr": "sin(x)",
  "range": ["-pi", "pi + A * t"],
  "fill_regions": [{ "above": "0" }]
}
```

Both range endpoints are live expressions â€” the curve extends in real time.

---

## Notes

- `expr`, `above`, `below`, `rightOf`, `leftOf`, and `opacity` all support **slider variables**, **time `t`**, and **scene functions**.
- `above`/`below` boundaries support `x` as a variable, making fill-between-curves natural.
- All four membership conditions (`above`, `below`, `rightOf`, `leftOf`) are consistent: each names the region the fill occupies, not where it clips.
- For static curves that never change, `parametric_curve` with `y`/`x` fields is simpler. Use `animated_curve` when interactivity or fill regions are needed.

steps.title: Basic curve â€” no fill
steps.description: A simple `animated_curve` plotting `y = AÂ·sin(x + Ď†)`. Drag the sliders to reshape the wave. No fill regions â€” just the curve line.

steps.title: Fill between curve and x-axis
steps.description: Same wave with a fill region that shades between the curve and the x-axis (y=0). Blue above zero, red below â€” each a separate fill region.

steps.title: Fill clipped by left/right bounds
steps.description: Fill region clipped to a horizontal window [L, R]. Drag the L and R sliders to move the shaded interval. The fill only appears where the x coordinate falls inside [L, R].

steps.title: Fill between two curves
steps.description: Fill the area between `sin(x)` (top) and `cos(x) - 1` (bottom), over the range [0, Ď€]. Uses `above` to clip where curve â‰Ą other curve.
